title: Component guideline
description: Per-component guideline JSON. Lives at components/src/guidelines/<slug>.json. Two shapes are accepted: (1) curated authored content (rich content_guidelines / design_guidelines / examples) and (2) auto-generated stubs (carry _stub:true, mostly-null content fields). The _index.json sibling has a distinct shape and is validated separately.

component.description: Display name of the component as it appears in Figma. May include emoji prefixes (e.g. ⛔️ for stubs). Stored as-authored; not slug-normalized.

_stub.description: Marks the file as an auto-generated stub (no curated content yet). Curated files omit this field or set it to false. CI uses this to skip stubs in coverage reports.

_stubGeneratedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the stub was auto-generated. Only meaningful when _stub is true.

page_id.description: Figma page node ID where this component's documentation lives. Used by the Figma sync to re-fetch frames.

extracted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent Figma extraction for this component.

frames_found.description: Names of documentation frames present in Figma for this component (Content guidelines, Design guidelines, Components, etc.).

frames_missing.description: Names of documentation frames NOT yet authored in Figma. Coverage signal for the design team.

content_guidelines.description: Voice + copy guidance for this component. null on stubs. Curated shape carries a sections[] array of headings/rules. Schema intentionally permissive — content shape varies across components.

design_guidelines.description: Visual + structural design conventions for this component. null on stubs. Curated shape varies — anatomy, do/don't, sizing rules, etc.

variants.description: Component variant matrix (axes + total count). Populated by Figma sync from the component-set property definitions.

examples.description: Ready-made usage examples. Curated content links to Figma frames or describes scenarios.

behavior.description: Interaction + state-transition behavior notes. Often references motion patterns by slug (Phase 2 convention).

notes.description: Free-form author notes — caveats, open questions, links to source-of-truth Figma frames.
examples: See Figma frame XYZ for canonical anatomy.
